channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: Erased, But Not Forgotten: Erased Rectified Flow Transformers Still Remain Unsafe Under Concept Attack
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ReFlux, an attack specifically designed to reactivate suppressed concepts in rectified-flow text-to-image transformers (e.g., Flux) that had undergone concept erasure.
  • Targets attention localization via a reverse-attention optimization, reinforced by a velocity-guided dynamic and a consistency-preserving objective to restore erased signals while keeping global layout and unrelated content stable.
  • Provides extensive experiments demonstrating the attack's effectiveness and efficiency, establishing a benchmark to evaluate robustness of concept-erasure defenses in next-generation T2I models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nanxiang Jiang', 'Zhaoxin Fan', 'Enhan Kang', 'Daiheng Gao', 'Yun Zhou', 'Yanxia Chang', 'Zheng Zhu', 'Yeying Jin', 'Wenjun Wu']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attack', 'concept erasure bypass', 'text-to-image safety', 'attention-targeted attack', 'robustness evaluation']

item.title: On Robustness of Vision-Language-Action Model against Multi-Modal Perturbations
item.description:
Summary

  • Evaluates robustness of vision-language-action (VLA) models under 17 perturbations across four modalities and finds actions are the most fragile modality; existing visual-robust VLAs do not generalize robustness to other modalities.
  • Proposes RobustVLA: output robustness via offline robust optimization against worst-case action noise (adversarial training / label smoothing / outlier penalization) and input robustness via action consistency across semantic-preserving input variations.
  • Uses a multi-armed bandit (UCB) to identify the most harmful perturbations for training, and reports substantial gains on LIBERO and on a real-world FR5 robot, plus faster inference than prior visual-robust baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jianing Guo', 'Zhenhong Wu', 'Chang Tu', 'Yiyao Ma', 'Xiangqi Kong', 'Zhiqian Liu', 'Jiaming Ji', 'Shuning Zhang', 'Yuanpei Chen', 'Kai Chen', 'Qi Dou', 'Yaodong Yang', 'Xianglong Liu', 'Huijie Zhao', 'Weifeng Lv', 'Simin Li']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'adversarial-training', 'multimodal-robustness', 'vision-language-action']

item.title: Enhancing Out-of-Distribution Detection with Extended Logit Normalization
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes ELogitNorm, an extension of Logit Normalization that adds a feature distance-awareness loss to address feature collapse.
  • A hyperparameter-free training formulation intended to improve post-hoc OOD scoring methods while preserving in-distribution classification accuracy and calibration.
  • Empirical evaluation shows improved OOD detection over state-of-the-art training-time methods across standard benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yifan Ding', 'Xixi Liu', 'Jonas Unger', 'Gabriel Eilertsen']
  • Tags: ['out-of-distribution detection', 'robustness', 'model calibration', 'training-time defenses', 'logit normalization']

item.title: VII: Visual Instruction Injection for Jailbreaking Image-to-Video Generation Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Visual Instruction Injection (VII), a training-free, transferable jailbreaking framework that encodes malicious intent into safe-looking reference images to bypass image-to-video (I2V) safety filters.
  • VII consists of a Malicious Intent Reprogramming module to distill unsafe text prompts and a Visual Instruction Grounding module to render those intents as visual instructions on benign images.
  • Evaluated on four commercial I2V models (Kling-v2.5-turbo, Gemini Veo-3.1, Seedance-1.5-pro, PixVerse-V5), achieving up to 83.5% attack success and near-zero refusal rates, outperforming baselines.
  • Focuses on jailbreak/adversarial attack methodology and empirical red-teaming of multimodal generation systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bowen Zheng', 'Yongli Xiang', 'Ziming Hong', 'Zerong Lin', 'Chaojian Yu', 'Tongliang Liu', 'Xinge You']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'visual prompt injection', 'adversarial attack', 'model safety', 'red teaming']

item.title: When Safety Collides: Resolving Multi-Category Harmful Conflicts in Text-to-Image Diffusion via Adaptive Safety Guidance
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Conflict-aware Adaptive Safety Guidance (CASG), a training-free framework to resolve multi-category harmful conflicts in text-to-image diffusion models by dynamically selecting a single category-aligned safety direction during generation.
  • CASG consists of two components: Conflict-aware Category Identification (CaCI) to detect which harm category is most aligned with the model's evolving generative state, and Conflict-resolving Guidance Application (CrGA) to apply safety steering only along that identified category to avoid cross-category interference.
  • Applicable to both latent-space and text-space safety mechanisms; experiments on T2I safety benchmarks report up to a 15.4% reduction in harmful rate versus prior methods that average safety directions across categories.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yongli Xiang', 'Ziming Hong', 'Zhaoqing Wang', 'Xiangyu Zhao', 'Bo Han', 'Tongliang Liu']
  • Tags: ['safety-guidance', 'text-to-image', 'defense', 'harmful-content-mitigation', 'diffusion-models']

item.title: RecoverMark: Robust Watermarking for Localization and Recovery of Manipulated Faces
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes RecoverMark, a robust watermarking framework that embeds a face's own content into the surrounding background to enable manipulation localization, content recovery, and ownership verification simultaneously.
  • Designs a two-stage training paradigm with distortion layers that simulate diverse attacks (including watermark removal) and a progressive training strategy to improve robustness against seen and unseen attacks.
  • Emphasizes real-world constraints (attackers must preserve background semantics) and demonstrates generalizability to in-distribution and out-of-distribution data.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Haonan An', 'Xiaohui Ye', 'Guang Hua', 'Yihang Tao', 'Hangcheng Cao', 'Xiangyu Yu', 'Yuguang Fang']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'image forensics', 'tamper localization', 'robustness', 'ownership verification']

item.title: AIForge-Doc: A Benchmark for Detecting AI-Forged Tampering in Financial and Form Documents
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces AIForge-Doc, a pixel-annotated benchmark of 4,061 document images forged via diffusion-model inpainting (Gemini 2.5 Flash Image, Ideogram v2 Edit) targeting numeric fields in receipts/forms across 9 languages.
  • Evaluates state-of-the-art detectors (TruFor, DocTamper) and a zero-shot GPT-4o judge, showing dramatic performance drops (e.g., DocTamper AUC 0.563, IoU 0.020; GPT-4o ~0.509), demonstrating AI-forged inpainting is largely undetected by current methods.
  • Provides DocTamper-compatible pixel masks and uses four public datasets (CORD, WildReceipt, SROIE, XFUND), framing AI inpainting-based document forgery as a new, unsolved forensics challenge.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiaqi Wu', 'Yuchen Zhou', 'Muduo Xu', 'Zisheng Liang', 'Simiao Ren', 'Jiayu Xue', 'Meige Yang', 'Siying Chen', 'Jingheng Huan']
  • Tags: ['document forgery', 'image inpainting', 'forensics benchmark', 'deepfake detection', 'adversarial attack (tampering)']

item.title: BiRQA: Bidirectional Robust Quality Assessment for Images
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BiRQA, a compact full-reference image quality assessment model with bidirectional multiscale feature fusion for speed and accuracy.
  • Introduces Anchored Adversarial Training, a theoretically grounded defense using clean anchors and a ranking loss to bound prediction error under attacks.
  • Demonstrates improved robustness under unseen white-box attacks (SROCC improvements) while matching or exceeding SOTA accuracy and running ~3x faster.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Aleksandr Gushchin', 'Dmitriy S. Vatolin', 'Anastasia Antsiferova']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'adversarial training', 'image quality assessment', 'white-box attacks', 'defense mechanisms']

item.title: Intent Laundering: AI Safety Datasets Are Not What They Seem
item.description:
Summary

  • Evaluates widely used AI safety datasets and finds they overrely on overt 'triggering cues' (words/phrases) that provoke model refusals rather than reflecting real adversarial intent.
  • Introduces 'intent laundering', a procedure that removes triggering cues while preserving malicious intent, demonstrating datasets fail to represent real-world attacks when cues are abstracted away.
  • Shows intent laundering can be used as a practical black-box jailbreaking technique with high success rates (≈90–98%), rendering otherwise 'safe' models (e.g., Gemini 3 Pro, Claude Sonnet 3.7) vulnerable.
  • Concludes current safety evaluation practices mischaracterize adversarial behavior and calls for more realistic red-teaming/dataset design.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shahriar Golchin', 'Marc Wetter']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attacks', 'jailbreaking', 'dataset evaluation', 'red teaming', 'safety benchmarks']

item.title: MCPShield: A Security Cognition Layer for Adaptive Trust Calibration in Model Context Protocol Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a security misalignment in the Model Context Protocol (MCP) where agentic LLMs implicitly trust third-party MCP servers, exposing them to MCP-based attacks throughout the tool invocation lifecycle.
  • Proposes MCPShield, a plug-in security cognition layer that performs metadata-guided probing before invocation, constrains execution at runtime, and updates security cognition via post-invocation trace reasoning.
  • Evaluates MCPShield across six novel MCP-based attack scenarios and six widely used agentic LLMs, reporting strong generalization, low false positives on benign servers, and low deployment overhead.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhenhong Zhou', 'Yuanhe Zhang', 'Hongwei Cai', 'Moayad Aloqaily', 'Ouns Bouachir', 'Linsey Pang', 'Prakhar Mehrotra', 'Kun Wang', 'Qingsong Wen']
  • Tags: ['Tool Invocation Security', 'LLM Agents', 'Runtime Monitoring', 'Defense Mechanism', 'Red-Teaming/Evaluation']

item.title: Watermarking Language Models with Error Correcting Codes
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Robust Binary Code (RBC) watermarking for language model outputs using error-correcting codes to embed detectable signals with minimal quality loss.
  • Provides an information-theoretic framing, a statistical test for detection (with p-values), and theoretical guarantees on detectability and robustness.
  • Empirically evaluates on base and instruction-tuned models, showing robustness to edits, deletions, and translations and favorable performance compared to prior methods.
  • Focuses on practical, fast watermark generation and robust detection — a defense mechanism against misuse/undetectable machine-generated text.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Patrick Chao', 'Yan Sun', 'Edgar Dobriban', 'Hamed Hassani']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'model provenance', 'robustness', 'detection', 'error-correcting-codes']

item.title: What Matters For Safety Alignment?
item.description:
Summary

  • Large-scale empirical evaluation of safety alignment across 32 LLMs/LRMs and 13 model families (3B–235B params) using five safety datasets, 56 jailbreak techniques, and four chain-of-thought (CoT) attack strategies (4.6M API calls).
  • Finds models with integrated reasoning/self-reflection (e.g., GPT-OSS-20B, Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Thinking, GPT-OSS-120B) are safest, and that post-training/knowledge distillation can degrade safety alignment unless safety is explicitly optimized.
  • Demonstrates a major vulnerability: CoT attacks via response prefixes drastically increase jailbreak success (avg 3.34x; up to 0.6%→96.3%), highlighting risks of text-completion interfaces and user-defined response prefixes.
  • Identifies roleplay, prompt injection, and gradient-based adversarial prompt search as the dominant methods for eliciting unaligned behaviors in modern models.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xing Li', 'Hui-Ling Zhen', 'Lihao Yin', 'Xianzhi Yu', 'Zhenhua Dong', 'Mingxuan Yuan']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial attacks', 'safety alignment', 'red teaming', 'LLM robustness']

item.title: Refusal Steering: Fine-grained Control over LLM Refusal Behaviour for Sensitive Topics
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Refusal Steering, an inference-time activation-steering method to finely control LLM refusal behaviour on politically sensitive topics without retraining.
  • Uses an LLM-as-a-judge to assign refusal confidence scores and derives ridge-regularized steering vectors that isolate a refusal–compliance direction; can both remove and induce targeted refusals.
  • Evaluates across 4B–80B models (e.g., Qwen3-Next-80B), showing removal of political refusal while retaining safety on JailbreakBench and near-baseline general benchmark performance; analyzes layer/dimensional concentration of refusal signals.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Iker Garc\\'ia-Ferrero", 'David Montero', 'Roman Orus']
  • Tags: ['activation steering', 'inference-time defense', 'model alignment', 'moderation/control', 'jailbreak robustness']

item.title: Personal Information Parroting in Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces an improved regex-and-rules (R&R) detector suite for identifying personal information (emails, phone numbers, IPs) in training data.
  • Measures memorization in the Pythia model suite: finds 13.6% of 483 curated PI instances are exactly parroted by Pythia-6.9B when prompted with preceding context.
  • Shows memorization increases with model size and pretraining steps (160M–6.9B models; 70k–143k iterations); smallest model still parrots 2.7% of PI.
  • Recommends aggressive filtering and anonymization of pretraining datasets to mitigate personal information parroting.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nishant Subramani', 'Kshitish Ghate', 'Mona Diab']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'memorization', 'training-data-leakage', 'data-filtering', 'PI-detection']

item.title: No One Size Fits All: QueryBandits for Hallucination Mitigation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces QueryBandits, a model-agnostic contextual bandit framework that adaptively selects query-rewrite strategies to mitigate LLM hallucinations.
  • Operates online with an empirically validated reward function and uses Thompson Sampling; works with closed-source models via forward-pass rewrites (no retraining).
  • Evaluated across 16 QA scenarios: achieves 87.5% win rate vs. No-Rewrite and substantially outperforms static zero-shot policies; shows contextual policies beat vanilla bandits.
  • Finds some static rewrite policies can increase hallucinations (higher cumulative regret), supporting the need for adaptive, context-aware strategies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nicole Cho', 'William Watson', 'Alec Koppel', 'Sumitra Ganesh', 'Manuela Veloso']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'prompt/query rewriting', 'contextual bandits', 'LLM safety/defense', 'closed-source model deployment']

item.title: ConceptRM: The Quest to Mitigate Alert Fatigue through Consensus-Based Purity-Driven Data Cleaning for Reflection Modelling
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes ConceptRM, a method to construct high-quality training corpora for reflection models that filter/intercept false alerts to mitigate alert fatigue.
  • Uses a small set of expert-annotated anchors to generate perturbed datasets with varying noise ratios and trains multiple models via co-teaching for collaborative learning.
  • Identifies reliable negative samples through consensus among distinct models to clean noisy production feedback data with minimal annotation cost.
  • Reports substantial improvements over LLM baselines on both in-domain and out-of-domain datasets for intercepting false alerts.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yongda Yu', 'Lei Zhang', 'Xinxin Guo', 'Minghui Yu', 'Zhengqi Zhuang', 'Guoping Rong', 'Haifeng Shen', 'Zhengfeng Li', 'Boge Wang', 'Guoan Zhang', 'Bangyu Xiang', 'Xiaobin Xu']
  • Tags: ['data-cleaning', 'label-noise-robustness', 'safety/filtering', 'co-teaching']

item.title: Skill-Inject: Measuring Agent Vulnerability to Skill File Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents SkillInject, a benchmark of 202 skill-file prompt injection attack/task pairs targeting LLM agents (ranging from overtly malicious to subtle context-dependent injections).
  • Evaluates widely-used frontier LLM agents on both security (avoiding harmful instructions) and utility (complying with legitimate instructions), finding up to 80% attack success and execution of highly harmful behaviors.
  • Shows that scaling/models alone or simple input filtering are insufficient mitigations and argues for context-aware authorization frameworks for robust agent security.
  • Provides the benchmark and tooling publicly to enable systematic testing of agent vulnerabilities to skill-based prompt injection.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['David Schmotz', 'Luca Beurer-Kellner', 'Sahar Abdelnabi', 'Maksym Andriushchenko']
  • Tags: ['prompt-injection', 'agent-security', 'benchmark', 'third-party-skills', 'data-exfiltration']

item.title: Intent Laundering: AI Safety Datasets Are Not What They Seem
item.description:
Summary

  • Evaluates widely used AI safety datasets and finds they overrely on overt 'triggering cues' (words/phrases) that provoke model refusals rather than reflecting real adversarial intent.
  • Introduces 'intent laundering', a procedure that removes triggering cues while preserving malicious intent, demonstrating datasets fail to represent real-world attacks when cues are abstracted away.
  • Shows intent laundering can be used as a practical black-box jailbreaking technique with high success rates (≈90–98%), rendering otherwise 'safe' models (e.g., Gemini 3 Pro, Claude Sonnet 3.7) vulnerable.
  • Concludes current safety evaluation practices mischaracterize adversarial behavior and calls for more realistic red-teaming/dataset design.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shahriar Golchin', 'Marc Wetter']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attacks', 'jailbreaking', 'dataset evaluation', 'red teaming', 'safety benchmarks']

item.title: Sharp Gaussian approximations for Decentralized Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proves a Berry–Esseen theorem for final iterates of decentralized/local SGD, enabling valid multiplier bootstrap inference for those estimates.
  • Develops two time-uniform Gaussian approximation results for the full trajectory of local SGD, aimed at robustness analysis.
  • Uses the time-uniform approximations to justify Gaussian bootstrap–based statistical tests for detecting adversarial attacks on federated/decentralized training.
  • Provides extensive simulations to validate the theoretical approximations and the proposed attack-detection procedures.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Soham Bonnerjee', 'Sayar Karmakar', 'Wei Biao Wu']
  • Tags: ['federated learning', 'local SGD', 'robustness', 'adversarial attack detection', 'statistical bootstrap']

item.title: Watermarking Language Models with Error Correcting Codes
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Robust Binary Code (RBC) watermarking for language model outputs using error-correcting codes to embed detectable signals with minimal quality loss.
  • Provides an information-theoretic framing, a statistical test for detection (with p-values), and theoretical guarantees on detectability and robustness.
  • Empirically evaluates on base and instruction-tuned models, showing robustness to edits, deletions, and translations and favorable performance compared to prior methods.
  • Focuses on practical, fast watermark generation and robust detection — a defense mechanism against misuse/undetectable machine-generated text.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Patrick Chao', 'Yan Sun', 'Edgar Dobriban', 'Hamed Hassani']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'model provenance', 'robustness', 'detection', 'error-correcting-codes']

item.title: SAGE-5GC: Security-Aware Guidelines for Evaluating Anomaly Detection in the 5G Core Network
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SAGE-5GC: security-aware evaluation guidelines for ML-based anomaly detectors in 5G Core networks, emphasizing realistic (non-IID, adversarial) deployment conditions.
  • Evaluates baseline detector performance on a realistic 5G Core dataset for PFCP control-plane attacks and studies detector sensitivity to attacker-controllable feature manipulations.
  • Develops and applies a black-box genetic-algorithm-based adversary that perturbs only controllable features while preserving attack functionality to evade detection.
  • Shows that adversarially crafted attacks substantially degrade detection performance, arguing for robustness-focused evaluation methodologies for 5G anomaly detection.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Cristian Manca', 'Christian Scano', 'Giorgio Piras', 'Fabio Brau', 'Maura Pintor', 'Battista Biggio']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attacks', 'anomaly detection', 'network security', '5G', 'robustness evaluation']

item.title: Towards Trustworthy GUI Agents: A Survey
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies the 'execution gap'—misalignment between perception, reasoning, and interaction—as the central trustworthiness challenge for GUI agents.
  • Presents a workflow-aligned taxonomy decomposing trust into Perception Trust, Reasoning Trust, and Interaction Trust, and analyzes how failures propagate through action/observation loops.
  • Systematically reviews benign failure modes, adversarial attacks, and defense mechanisms specific to GUI settings, and critiques current evaluation practices.
  • Highlights trust-aware metrics and benchmarks that capture error cascades and security/utility trade-offs, and outlines open challenges for safe deployment.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yucheng Shi', 'Wenhao Yu', 'Jingyuan Huang', 'Wenlin Yao', 'Wenhu Chen', 'Ninghao Liu']
  • Tags: ['GUI agents', 'Adversarial attacks', 'Defenses & robustness', 'Benchmarks & evaluation', 'Trust & safety']

item.title: Assessing the Impact of Speaker Identity in Speech Spoofing Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Investigates the influence of speaker identity on automatic speech spoofing detection systems and questions the assumption that embeddings are speaker-independent.
  • Proposes a Speaker-Invariant Multi-Task (SInMT) framework with two approaches: one that models speaker identity in embeddings and another that removes speaker information using multi-task learning and a gradient reversal layer.
  • Evaluates on four datasets and reports substantial improvements in anti-spoofing performance (average EER reduction ~17%, up to 48% for the hardest attacks).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Anh-Tuan Dao', 'Driss Matrouf', 'Nicholas Evans']
  • Tags: ['anti-spoofing', 'speaker recognition', 'multi-task learning', 'defense', 'audio security']

item.title: Personal Information Parroting in Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces an improved regex-and-rules (R&R) detector suite for identifying personal information (emails, phone numbers, IPs) in training data.
  • Measures memorization in the Pythia model suite: finds 13.6% of 483 curated PI instances are exactly parroted by Pythia-6.9B when prompted with preceding context.
  • Shows memorization increases with model size and pretraining steps (160M–6.9B models; 70k–143k iterations); smallest model still parrots 2.7% of PI.
  • Recommends aggressive filtering and anonymization of pretraining datasets to mitigate personal information parroting.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nishant Subramani', 'Kshitish Ghate', 'Mona Diab']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'memorization', 'training-data-leakage', 'data-filtering', 'PI-detection']

item.title: No One Size Fits All: QueryBandits for Hallucination Mitigation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces QueryBandits, a model-agnostic contextual bandit framework that adaptively selects query-rewrite strategies to mitigate LLM hallucinations.
  • Operates online with an empirically validated reward function and uses Thompson Sampling; works with closed-source models via forward-pass rewrites (no retraining).
  • Evaluated across 16 QA scenarios: achieves 87.5% win rate vs. No-Rewrite and substantially outperforms static zero-shot policies; shows contextual policies beat vanilla bandits.
  • Finds some static rewrite policies can increase hallucinations (higher cumulative regret), supporting the need for adaptive, context-aware strategies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nicole Cho', 'William Watson', 'Alec Koppel', 'Sumitra Ganesh', 'Manuela Veloso']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'prompt/query rewriting', 'contextual bandits', 'LLM safety/defense', 'closed-source model deployment']

item.title: Is the Trigger Essential? A Feature-Based Triggerless Backdoor Attack in Vertical Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a novel feature-based triggerless backdoor attack on vertical federated learning (VFL) that does not require a visible trigger at inference time.
  • Attacker model: honest-but-curious passive party; attack comprises label inference, poison generation (with amplification and perturbation), and backdoor execution.
  • Extensive experiments on five benchmarks show the attack outperforms baselines by 2–50x, preserves main-task performance, scales to many passive parties, and is robust to several defenses.
  • Highlights a new backdoor threat pathway in VFL and calls for revisiting defenses for practical VFL deployments.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yige Liu', 'Yiwei Lou', 'Che Wang', 'Yongzhi Cao', 'Hanpin Wang']
  • Tags: ['backdoor', 'vertical federated learning', 'triggerless attack', 'poisoning', 'label inference']

item.title: Oracle-Robust Online Alignment for Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces an oracle-robust formulation for online alignment of LLMs under misspecified preference feedback using a pointwise oracle uncertainty set and worst-case optimization.
  • For log-linear policies, derives an exact closed-form decomposition of the robust objective into the original loss plus an explicit sensitivity penalty, making the problem tractable.
  • Proposes projected stochastic composite updates for the resulting weakly convex objective and proves a ~O(ε^{-2}) oracle complexity to reach approximate stationarity.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zimeng Li', 'Mudit Gaur', 'Vaneet Aggarwal']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'alignment', 'preference learning', 'adversarial-robustness', 'online reinforcement learning']

item.title: CREDIT: Certified Ownership Verification of Deep Neural Networks Against Model Extraction Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes CREDIT, a certified ownership verification framework to detect model extraction attacks on MLaaS by measuring similarity between models.
  • Uses mutual information to quantify model similarity and derives a practical verification threshold with rigorous theoretical guarantees.
  • Evaluates the approach across multiple mainstream datasets/tasks and reports state-of-the-art performance; implementation released publicly.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bolin Shen', 'Zhan Cheng', 'Neil Zhenqiang Gong', 'Fan Yao', 'Yushun Dong']
  • Tags: ['model extraction', 'ownership verification', 'certified defense', 'mutual information', 'MLaaS security']

item.title: CITED: A Decision Boundary-Aware Signature for GNNs Towards Model Extraction Defense
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes CITED, a decision-boundary-aware signature method for ownership verification of GNNs to defend against model extraction attacks (MEAs).
  • Supports verification at both embedding and label output levels without degrading downstream performance or requiring auxiliary models.
  • Claims improved robustness and effectiveness compared with existing watermarking and fingerprinting defenses; validated via extensive experiments and released code.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bolin Shen', 'Md Shamim Seraj', 'Zhan Cheng', 'Shayok Chakraborty', 'Yushun Dong']
  • Tags: ['model extraction', 'ownership verification', 'graph neural networks', 'watermarking', 'defense']

item.title: Intent Laundering: AI Safety Datasets Are Not What They Seem
item.description:
Summary

  • Evaluates widely used AI safety datasets and finds they overrely on overt 'triggering cues' (words/phrases) that provoke model refusals rather than reflecting real adversarial intent.
  • Introduces 'intent laundering', a procedure that removes triggering cues while preserving malicious intent, demonstrating datasets fail to represent real-world attacks when cues are abstracted away.
  • Shows intent laundering can be used as a practical black-box jailbreaking technique with high success rates (≈90–98%), rendering otherwise 'safe' models (e.g., Gemini 3 Pro, Claude Sonnet 3.7) vulnerable.
  • Concludes current safety evaluation practices mischaracterize adversarial behavior and calls for more realistic red-teaming/dataset design.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shahriar Golchin', 'Marc Wetter']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attacks', 'jailbreaking', 'dataset evaluation', 'red teaming', 'safety benchmarks']

item.title: What Matters For Safety Alignment?
item.description:
Summary

  • Large-scale empirical evaluation of safety alignment across 32 LLMs/LRMs (3B–235B) using five safety datasets, 56 jailbreaks, and 4 CoT attack strategies (≈4.6M API calls).
  • Finds LRMs with integrated reasoning/self-reflection (e.g., GPT-OSS-20B, Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Thinking, GPT-OSS-120B) are among the safest, suggesting reasoning/self-reflection aids robustness.
  • Shows post-training and knowledge distillation can degrade safety alignment, arguing safety must be an explicit objective during these stages.
  • Identifies a major vulnerability: CoT attacks via response prefixes dramatically increase attack success (avg 3.34x; up to 96.3% in one model); primary attack vectors are roleplay, prompt injection, and gradient-based adversarial prompt search.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xing Li', 'Hui-Ling Zhen', 'Lihao Yin', 'Xianzhi Yu', 'Zhenhua Dong', 'Mingxuan Yuan']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'prompt-injection', 'chain-of-thought-attacks', 'safety-evaluation', 'adversarial-prompting']

item.title: Refusal Steering: Fine-grained Control over LLM Refusal Behaviour for Sensitive Topics
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Refusal Steering, an inference-time activation-steering method to finely control LLM refusal behaviour on politically sensitive topics without retraining.
  • Uses an LLM-as-a-judge to assign refusal confidence scores and derives ridge-regularized steering vectors that isolate a refusal–compliance direction; can both remove and induce targeted refusals.
  • Evaluates across 4B–80B models (e.g., Qwen3-Next-80B), showing removal of political refusal while retaining safety on JailbreakBench and near-baseline general benchmark performance; analyzes layer/dimensional concentration of refusal signals.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Iker Garc\\'ia-Ferrero", 'David Montero', 'Roman Orus']
  • Tags: ['activation steering', 'inference-time defense', 'model alignment', 'moderation/control', 'jailbreak robustness']

item.title: On Robustness of Vision-Language-Action Model against Multi-Modal Perturbations
item.description:
Summary

  • Evaluates robustness of vision-language-action (VLA) models under 17 perturbations across four modalities and finds actions are the most fragile modality; existing visual-robust VLAs do not generalize robustness to other modalities.
  • Proposes RobustVLA: output robustness via offline robust optimization against worst-case action noise (adversarial training / label smoothing / outlier penalization) and input robustness via action consistency across semantic-preserving input variations.
  • Uses a multi-armed bandit (UCB) to identify the most harmful perturbations for training, and reports substantial gains on LIBERO and on a real-world FR5 robot, plus faster inference than prior visual-robust baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jianing Guo', 'Zhenhong Wu', 'Chang Tu', 'Yiyao Ma', 'Xiangqi Kong', 'Zhiqian Liu', 'Jiaming Ji', 'Shuning Zhang', 'Yuanpei Chen', 'Kai Chen', 'Qi Dou', 'Yaodong Yang', 'Xianglong Liu', 'Huijie Zhao', 'Weifeng Lv', 'Simin Li']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'adversarial-training', 'multimodal-robustness', 'vision-language-action']

item.title: Evaluating and Mitigating LLM-as-a-judge Bias in Communication Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematic study of judgment biases in LLM-as-a-judge models (GPT-Judge and JudgeLM) under point-wise scoring across 11 implicit/explicit bias types.
  • Finds LLM judges generally robust to biased inputs (they score biased samples lower) and that providing a detailed rubric improves robustness.
  • Shows that fine-tuning judges on high-scoring but biased responses can substantially degrade performance, and observed score distributions vary with task difficulty.
  • Proposes four mitigation strategies to promote fair and reliable AI judging in practical communication scenarios.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiaxin Gao', 'Chen Chen', 'Yanwen Jia', 'Xueluan Gong', 'Kwok-Yan Lam', 'Qian Wang']
  • Tags: ['LLM-as-judge', 'evaluation bias', 'robustness', 'mitigation', 'training-data vulnerability']

item.title: "Are You Sure?": An Empirical Study of Human Perception Vulnerability in LLM-Driven Agentic Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Defines and studies Agent-Mediated Deception (AMD), where compromised LLM-driven agents deceive human users, and introduces HAT-Lab, a research platform for high-fidelity human-agent experiments.
  • Runs a large-scale empirical user study (303 participants) across nine scenarios (everyday and professional domains) finding high human susceptibility (only 8.6% perceived AMD), six cognitive failure modes, and surprising patterns like increased susceptibility among some domain experts.
  • Evaluates defenses and mitigation strategies: effective warnings must interrupt workflows with low verification cost, and experiential learning via HAT-Lab markedly increases user caution (>90% who perceived risks report increased caution).

Paper Type

  • Research (empirical user study)

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xinfeng Li', 'Shenyu Dai', 'Kelong Zheng', 'Yue Xiao', 'Gelei Deng', 'Wei Dong', 'Xiaofeng Wang']
  • Tags: ['agent-mediated deception', 'human-targeted attacks', 'user study', 'defenses / warnings', 'social engineering']

item.title: SoK: Agentic Skills -- Beyond Tool Use in LLM Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematization of agentic skills: defines lifecycle (discovery, practice, distillation, storage, composition, evaluation, update) and two taxonomies (seven design patterns; representation × scope).
  • Analyzes security and governance implications of skill-based agents, including supply-chain risks, prompt injection via skill payloads, trust-tiered execution, and a case study of a large-scale malicious-skill campaign (ClawHavoc) that exfiltrated credentials and keys.
  • Surveys evaluation approaches and empirical findings on skill utility (curated skills often help; self-generated skills can degrade performance) and outlines open challenges for robust, verifiable, and certifiable skills.

Paper Type

  • Survey/Systematization

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yanna Jiang', 'Delong Li', 'Haiyu Deng', 'Baihe Ma', 'Xu Wang', 'Qin Wang', 'Guangsheng Yu']
  • Tags: ['Agent security', 'Supply-chain attacks', 'Prompt injection', 'Malicious skills', 'Security governance']

item.title: AdapTools: Adaptive Tool-based Indirect Prompt Injection Attacks on Agentic LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces AdapTools, an adaptive framework for indirect prompt injection (IPI) attacks against agentic LLMs that leverage external tools/services (e.g., MCP).
  • Proposes two components: (1) Adaptive Attack Strategy Construction to create transferable adversarial prompt strategies, and (2) Attack Enhancement to select stealthy tools that bypass task-relevance defenses.
  • Reports empirical gains: ~2.13× improvement in attack success rate and ~1.78× degradation in system utility, maintaining effectiveness against state-of-the-art defenses.
  • Provides a structured evaluation methodology for rigorous security assessment of tool-using LLM agents.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Che Wang', 'Jiaming Zhang', 'Ziqi Zhang', 'Zijie Wang', 'Yinghui Wang', 'Jianbo Gao', 'Tao Wei', 'Zhong Chen', 'Wei Yang Bryan Lim']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'tool-based attacks', 'adversarial attacks', 'red teaming']

item.title: OptiLeak: Efficient Prompt Reconstruction via Reinforcement Learning in Multi-tenant LLM Services
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies and exploits cache-based side-channel vulnerabilities in multi-tenant LLM serving to perform prompt reconstruction (prompt leakage).
  • Presents OptiLeak: a two-stage fine-tuning framework using reinforcement learning and automated identification of domain-specific 'hard tokens' to build preference pairs for Direct Preference Optimization.
  • Evaluates on medical and financial benchmarks, reporting up to 12.48× reduction in average requests per token across 3B–14B models, highlighting more severe privacy risks and recommending robust cache isolation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Longxiang Wang', 'Xiang Zheng', 'Xuhao Zhang', 'Yao Zhang', 'Ye Wu', 'Cong Wang']
  • Tags: ['prompt leakage', 'cache side-channel', 'privacy attack', 'reinforcement learning', 'LLM service security']

item.title: Personal Information Parroting in Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces an improved regex-and-rules (R&R) detector suite for identifying personal information (emails, phone numbers, IPs) in training data.
  • Measures memorization in the Pythia model suite: finds 13.6% of 483 curated PI instances are exactly parroted by Pythia-6.9B when prompted with preceding context.
  • Shows memorization increases with model size and pretraining steps (160M–6.9B models; 70k–143k iterations); smallest model still parrots 2.7% of PI.
  • Recommends aggressive filtering and anonymization of pretraining datasets to mitigate personal information parroting.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nishant Subramani', 'Kshitish Ghate', 'Mona Diab']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'memorization', 'training-data-leakage', 'data-filtering', 'PI-detection']

item.title: No One Size Fits All: QueryBandits for Hallucination Mitigation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces QueryBandits, a model-agnostic contextual bandit framework that adaptively selects query-rewrite strategies to mitigate LLM hallucinations.
  • Operates online with an empirically validated reward function and uses Thompson Sampling; works with closed-source models via forward-pass rewrites (no retraining).
  • Evaluated across 16 QA scenarios: achieves 87.5% win rate vs. No-Rewrite and substantially outperforms static zero-shot policies; shows contextual policies beat vanilla bandits.
  • Finds some static rewrite policies can increase hallucinations (higher cumulative regret), supporting the need for adaptive, context-aware strategies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nicole Cho', 'William Watson', 'Alec Koppel', 'Sumitra Ganesh', 'Manuela Veloso']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'prompt/query rewriting', 'contextual bandits', 'LLM safety/defense', 'closed-source model deployment']

item.title: CodeHacker: Automated Test Case Generation for Detecting Vulnerabilities in Competitive Programming Solutions
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents CodeHacker, an automated agent framework that generates adversarial test cases to break competitive programming solutions using strategies like stress testing, anti-hash attacks, and logic-specific targeting.
  • Introduces a Calibration Phase where the agent refines its Validator and Checker via self-generated adversarial probes to ensure validity and reliability of generated tests.
  • Empirically shows CodeHacker increases True Negative Rate (finding incorrect solutions that previously passed) and that the adversarial cases improve RL-trained model performance on benchmarks such as LiveCodeBench.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jingwei Shi', 'Xinxiang Yin', 'Jing Huang', 'Jinman Zhao', 'Shengyu Tao']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-test-generation', 'vulnerability-detection', 'automated-red-teaming', 'robustness-benchmarks', 'software-security']

item.title: OpenPort Protocol: A Security Governance Specification for AI Agent Tool Access
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes OpenPort Protocol (OPP), a governance-first specification for secure server-side gateway access to application tools by AI agents, emphasizing least-privilege, controlled writes, abuse resistance, and auditability.
  • Defines authorization-dependent discovery, a combined integration-credential/scoped-permissions/ABAC model, stable machine-actionable response envelopes, and risk-gated lifecycles that default writes to draft + human review with optional time-bounded auto-execution.
  • Specifies high-risk safeguards (preflight impact binding, idempotency), an optional State Witness profile to mitigate TOCTOU drift, and operational controls (admission control/rate limits, stable 429 semantics, structured audit events).
  • Provides a reference runtime and executable governance toolchain including layered conformance profiles, negative security tests, fuzz/abuse regression, and release-gate scans with reproducible validation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Genliang Zhu', 'Chu Wang', 'Ziyuan Wang', 'Zhida Li', 'Qiang Li']
  • Tags: ['access-control', 'agent-safety', 'governance', 'auditability', 'defense']

item.title: When Backdoors Go Beyond Triggers: Semantic Drift in Diffusion Models Under Encoder Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows encoder-side poisoning of text-to-image models can induce persistent, trigger-free semantic corruption that reshapes the representation manifold.
  • Provides a Jacobian-based geometric analysis revealing that backdoors act as low-rank, target-centered deformations that amplify local sensitivity and propagate distortion across semantic neighborhoods.
  • Introduces SEMAD (Semantic Alignment and Drift), a diagnostic framework to quantify internal embedding drift and downstream functional misalignment.
  • Validates results across diffusion and contrastive paradigms and argues for geometric audits beyond simple trigger activation and visual-fidelity metrics.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shenyang Chen', 'Liuwan Zhu']
  • Tags: ['backdoor', 'encoder poisoning', 'semantic drift', 'diffusion models', 'robustness/diagnostics']

item.title: ConceptRM: The Quest to Mitigate Alert Fatigue through Consensus-Based Purity-Driven Data Cleaning for Reflection Modelling
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes ConceptRM, a method to construct high-quality training corpora for reflection models that filter/intercept false alerts to mitigate alert fatigue.
  • Uses a small set of expert-annotated anchors to generate perturbed datasets with varying noise ratios and trains multiple models via co-teaching for collaborative learning.
  • Identifies reliable negative samples through consensus among distinct models to clean noisy production feedback data with minimal annotation cost.
  • Reports substantial improvements over LLM baselines on both in-domain and out-of-domain datasets for intercepting false alerts.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yongda Yu', 'Lei Zhang', 'Xinxin Guo', 'Minghui Yu', 'Zhengqi Zhuang', 'Guoping Rong', 'Haifeng Shen', 'Zhengfeng Li', 'Boge Wang', 'Guoan Zhang', 'Bangyu Xiang', 'Xiaobin Xu']
  • Tags: ['data-cleaning', 'label-noise-robustness', 'safety/filtering', 'co-teaching']

item.title: ICON: Indirect Prompt Injection Defense for Agents based on Inference-Time Correction
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ICON, a probing-to-mitigation framework to defend LLM agents from Indirect Prompt Injection (IPI) without over-refusal.
  • Detects IPI via a Latent Space Trace Prober that identifies over-focusing signatures, then applies a Mitigating Rectifier to surgically steer attention (manipulating query-key dependencies) and amplify task-relevant signals.
  • Reports strong empirical results: ~0.4% attack success rate and >50% task utility gain, robustness to OOD inputs, and extension to multimodal agents.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Che Wang', 'Fuyao Zhang', 'Jiaming Zhang', 'Ziqi Zhang', 'Yinghui Wang', 'Longtao Huang', 'Jianbo Gao', 'Zhong Chen', 'Wei Yang Bryan Lim']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'adversarial defense', 'agent security', 'latent-space detection']

item.title: When can we trust untrusted monitoring? A safety case sketch across collusion strategies
item.description:
Summary

  • Develops a taxonomy of collusion strategies that a misaligned model might use to subvert untrusted monitoring, including passive self-recognition, causal collusion, acausal collusion, and combined strategies.
  • Provides a safety case sketch for justifying untrusted monitoring deployments, relaxing prior assumptions and making explicit the assumptions required for pre-deployment testing to be meaningful.
  • Analyzes conditions under which passive self-recognition could be a more effective collusion strategy than previously studied attacks, and highlights unsolved challenges for robust evaluation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nelson Gardner-Challis', 'Jonathan Bostock', 'Georgiy Kozhevnikov', 'Morgan Sinclaire', 'Joan Velja', 'Alessandro Abate', 'Charlie Griffin']
  • Tags: ['untrusted monitoring', 'AI safety', 'adversarial strategies', 'red teaming', 'defense mechanisms']
